input.title: Multi-Vendor eCommerce Marketplace
input.description: Multi-Vendor eCommerce Marketplace is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with Next.js and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Node.js, MongoDB, Elasticsearch, Stripe for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Vendor Registration
requirements: Apply to become a seller with business details

FR-02.title: Vendor Store Setup
requirements: Customize storefront with logo and banner

FR-10.title: Vendor Payouts
requirements: Payout to bank account or PayPal

FR-11.title: Product Search
requirements: Filter by price, rating, vendor, category

FR-13.title: Dispute Resolution
requirements: Buyer opens dispute for order issues

FR-17.title: Wishlist
requirements: Price drop notifications for saved items

FR-23.title: Inventory Sync
requirements: Vendor syncs inventory from external system

FR-24.title: Mobile App

FR-01.title: Plan Vendor Registration Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for vendor registration and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Apply to become a seller with business details; Document verification for business license; Multi-step onboarding wizard.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for vendor registration is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for vendor registration are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for vendor registration can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Vendor Registration Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for vendor registration on Next.js, Node.js, MongoDB, Elasticsearch. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Apply to become a seller with business details; Document verification for business license; Multi-step onboarding wizard.
acceptance_criteria: Core vendor registration business rules are implemented for Multi-Vendor eCommerce Marketplace
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for vendor registration
API/service behavior for vendor registration is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Vendor Registration Frontend Flow
